NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 90 MPH IN ADDISON VERMONT. MULTIPLE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED WITH MANY LARGE LIMBS THROWN ABOUT. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURED AT A METAL STRUCTURE THAT COLLAPSED IN ON ITSELF. IN ADDITION, A STREET SIGN WAS HEAVILY MANGLED BY THE TORNADO. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 2 MINUTES AND FOR 1 MILE IN LENGTH WITH A MAXIMUM DIAMETER OF 50 YARDS.
TREES AND PROPERTY DAMAGE FROM PHOTOS RECEIVED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ROUTE 17 AND 22A.

AN EF-0 HAS BEEN CONFIRMED BY PUBLIC VIDEO. MINOR TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED ON THE VIDEO FOR A LITTE LESS THAN 2 MINUTES. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 70 MPH WITH A MAXIMUM WITH OF 25 YARDS. TIME AND LOCATION HAVE BEEN ESTIMATED FROM VIDEO AND RADAR.
